title: ficus phatnophylla var. meiocarpa
intro: Ficus phatnophylla var. meiocarpa, also known as the Thai banyan, is a lesser-known species of fig tree that originates from the tropical regions of Thailand. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments due to its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Ficus phatnophylla var. meiocarpa include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. In traditional herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of the plant are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
